features.title: Restore Linear/GitHub delivery pipeline
acceptance: Add ops/roadmap/features.json as the canonical machine-readable backlog registry.
Add a human-readable Linear/GitHub workflow doc for this repo.
Record Linear project/team, issue-key usage, labels, acceptance criteria, and verification expectations.
Update README or roadmap to point at the workflow doc.
Initial backlog records GitHub issue numbers and Linear issue keys after seeding.

features.title: Clean repo hygiene and add current status handoff
acceptance: git ls-files whisperforge-env | wc -l returns 0.
STATUS.md exists and names latest verification commands/results.
README or roadmap points to STATUS.md if appropriate.
No cache, venv, or generated artifacts are newly tracked.

features.title: Add browser-level Streamlit verification
acceptance: A documented command can render the app shell.
The command fails if Streamlit reports an exception or expected UI shell is missing.
Existing tests/smoke.sh still works.
Verification docs mention health smoke vs rendered UI smoke.

features.title: Bring services mode to pipeline parity
acceptance: Services mode accepts all modern pipeline options or clearly documents unsupported options.
Full PipelineResult fields can round-trip through HTTP adapters.
Storage can save modern Notion/export fields without dropping data.
Tests cover the payload contract.
verification: A docker-compose/service smoke test or documented local equivalent

features.title: Make long runs recoverable and retryable
acceptance: A partially completed run can be inspected from local artifacts.
Save/export retry does not duplicate or corrupt history unexpectedly.
Run history can link to local artifacts as well as Notion URLs.
Tests cover run-record/checkpoint behavior.

features.title: Add profile manifests and user-defined personas
acceptance: Existing profiles continue to work without a manifest.
prompts/<user>/profile.yaml can override defaults without code changes.
User-defined personas appear in the same UI selector as built-ins.
Tests cover manifest loading and persona precedence.
verification: Manual UI check: persona selector includes a test user-defined persona

features.title: Add source receipts and editorial eval fixtures
acceptance: Fixture evals can run without external credentials or clearly document credential requirements.
Source receipts are preserved in markdown/Notion where applicable.
Tests cover rendering/serialization of source receipts or eval metadata.
The feature does not make short/simple runs noisy.

features.title: Add deployment and operations commands
acceptance: One command runs the local unit suite.
One command runs health smoke.
One command starts the local app.
Services mode has an explicit smoke/run command or is documented as deferred.
Docs reference the command surface.

features.title: Build Wispr Flow voice inbox and capture handoff
acceptance: Add a capture inbox model with source type, created time, title, status, and linked run identifiers.
Add a fast paste/import path optimized for Wispr Flow output and other already-transcribed text.
Normalize captures before pipeline execution so audio, pasted transcript, and imported note flows share downstream metadata.
Persist capture metadata into local run artifacts and markdown/Notion export metadata where applicable.
Document the capture workflow in README or the roadmap docs.

features.title: Add knowledge-base inventory and health audit
acceptance: Add an inventory command or UI panel for profile knowledge-base files with filename, size, modified time, rough token count, and detected role.
Flag likely stale, duplicate, empty, oversized, or private/sensitive files using conservative heuristics.
Summarize profile-level health in a compact report that can be exported or copied.
Keep existing profile loading behavior unchanged for valid current profiles.
Document how agents should use the audit before changing prompts or KB docs.

features.title: Add source-grounded retrieval inspector
acceptance: Expose retrieved KB chunks per stage with source path, heading/offset when available, score, and reason/query context.
Show always-included voice anchors separately from semantic retrieval hits.
Persist retrieval metadata into run artifacts so it can be inspected after the session.
Add a compact UI or exported report that compares legacy KB dump vs RAG for the current capture/profile.
Keep Auto/Always/Never RAG behavior compatible with current settings.

features.title: Add prompt recipe manifests and command palette
acceptance: Define a minimal recipe manifest format for inputs, stages, provider/model defaults, KB mode, output sections, eval checks, and handoff targets.
Load profile-level and default recipes without breaking current manual controls.
Add a command palette or compact selector for running a recipe from a capture/current input.
Persist the recipe id and effective settings in run artifacts.
Include at least three built-in recipes covering article, brief/social, and issue/handoff workflows.

features.title: Build source-grounded composition studio
acceptance: Add a composition/review surface that displays drafted content with adjacent evidence or source receipts.
Expose claim flags, quotes, critique, revision notes, and compare/persona variants without hiding the primary draft.
Allow export/save flows to preserve evidence summaries with the final artifact.
Keep the existing output card workflow usable for simple runs.
Document the review workflow and its limitations.

features.title: Add modern transcription provider and privacy matrix
acceptance: Document current transcription backends and candidate providers in a matrix covering accuracy, streaming, timestamps, diarization, local/offline, vocabulary, cost, and privacy posture.
Add the matrix to docs and optionally expose a compact version in the UI.
Recommend default backend choices for local privacy, long-form accuracy, timestamps, and low-cost runs.
Identify the smallest next provider integration, if any, with explicit acceptance criteria.
Do not change runtime defaults without tests and documentation.

features.title: Add voice, grounding, and usefulness scorecards
acceptance: Define scorecard dimensions for voice, grounding, usefulness, recipe compliance, and handoff readiness.
Add fixture-based evals that can run without external credentials or clearly isolate credentialed checks.
Persist scorecard summaries in run artifacts and render them in markdown/Notion where appropriate.
Show a compact verdict in run history or output review.
Keep scorecards advisory; they should not block saves unless a recipe explicitly asks for it.

features.title: Create agentic handoff exports for issues and follow-ups
acceptance: Add an issue/handoff export format with context, acceptance criteria, tests/evals, verification, agent instructions, and out-of-scope sections.
Support generating GitHub/Linear-ready issue drafts from a capture or selected output.
Persist handoff drafts in run artifacts before external creation.
Include a dry-run preview so the user can review before creating external tracker records.
Document how this interacts with the existing agentic delivery contract.

features.title: Add run artifact reopen, resume, and retry UI
acceptance: Add a run browser that reads local manifests and shows status, created time, input type, recipe/settings, exports, and errors.
Allow reopening a run into the output/review surface from its manifest.
Allow retrying safe downstream handoffs such as markdown export or Notion save without re-running transcription/content stages.
Represent partial runs clearly and avoid corrupting history records.
Add docs for recovery behavior and known limits.

features.title: Upgrade profiles into a voice/project operating system
acceptance: Extend profile metadata to cover project context, recipe defaults, KB packs, privacy notes, and preferred handoff targets.
Keep existing profiles working without migration.
Add a profile summary surface or command that explains the effective profile configuration.
Add validation for missing referenced files and unsupported defaults.
Document the profile operating-system pattern with an example.

features.title: Add resurfacing digest for captures and generated outputs
acceptance: Define a digest format for notable captures, unresolved follow-ups, strong outputs, stale drafts, and reusable source nuggets.
Generate a local digest from run artifacts and capture metadata without requiring external services.
Link digest entries back to source runs, captures, and exports.
Keep the digest report-only unless the user explicitly asks for routing or publishing.
Document how often to run the digest and what it is safe to automate.

features.title: Prototype SongForge lyric and prompt-pack mode
acceptance: Add a SongForge recipe or mode that extracts themes, motifs, phrases, and emotional arc from a capture plus selected KB context.
Generate at least three structured outputs: lyric draft, spoken-word variant, and service-agnostic music prompt pack.
Include source notes or receipts explaining which transcript/KB moments informed key themes.
Keep generated lyrics original and based on user-provided/source material, not imitation of living artists.
Add documentation and fixture coverage for the creative workflow.

features.title: Finish public brand, repo metadata, and license decision
acceptance: GitHub repo description, topics, and homepage make it clear that spektorAI is the canonical WhisperForge implementation.
README/status docs point humans and agents to the canonical surfaces.
Decide whether to add a license to spektorAI; do not silently inherit the old whisperforge MIT license without human confirmation.
Legacy whisperforge README points to the canonical repo before archival.
No runtime code changes are included in this issue.
verification: Manual check: GitHub repo metadata reads as canonical WhisperForge.

features.title: Add watch-folder and import-folder capture intake
acceptance: Define a canonical import/watch-folder capture flow for audio files, transcripts, and text notes.
Capture records preserve source path, source type, created/imported timestamps, title/status, and linked run IDs.
Import behavior deduplicates obvious repeats and ignores generated chunk/temp files.
The UI or CLI clearly shows pending/imported captures before recipe execution.
Docs explain when to use Wispr Flow paste versus folder import.

features.title: Add weekly recaps and topic evolution summaries
acceptance: Generate a weekly recap from capture/run metadata with source links back to captures, transcripts, and outputs.
Generate a topic evolution summary from related captures/runs in chronological order.
Include people, action items, open questions, and next-week signals when present.
Keep the feature report-only unless a separate routing issue enables external publishing.
Add fixture coverage that runs without external credentials.

features.title: Evaluate FFmpeg large-file handling for the provider router
acceptance: Document whether FFmpeg chunking should become part of the canonical transcription router.
Identify which backends need large-file support, timestamps, diarization, streaming, and privacy fixtures.
Define file-size thresholds, chunking strategy, fallback behavior, and temp-file cleanup expectations.
Add or specify fixture coverage before changing runtime defaults.
Do not copy the old whisperforge monolith or Supabase assumptions.
verification: Any prototype must also run make test.

features.title: Add Obsidian-friendly markdown vault export
acceptance: Define a markdown vault export format for captures, transcripts, recaps, and generated outputs.
Preserve source receipts, run IDs, recipe metadata, tags, and links back to related captures/runs.
Avoid duplicating existing markdown export behavior unless a vault mode is explicitly selected.
Document the directory/index/tag conventions.
Add tests for serialization and path generation.

features.title: Run and document a real Wispr Flow-to-output dogfood session
acceptance: Run one real capture through capture/import, recipe run, review, markdown/Notion export, run reopen, and resurfacing digest.
Write a friction report in docs/dogfood with explicit blockers and missing controls.
Include concrete run artifact references and what was exported/published.
Create at least three follow-up issues from observed friction.

features.title: Pick next release target and align milestone scope
acceptance: Decide one milestone target and document rationale plus owner/date.
Publish mandatory-now vs deferred dependency map for that target.
Re-rank upcoming issue wave against the chosen target.
Keep roadmap and status docs consistent with the decision.

features.title: Extend approve-and-create routing to follow-up queue and digest destinations
acceptance: Add one new routing destination behind explicit approval.
Preserve dry-run fallback behavior and kill-switch semantics.
Add tests for success, missing-config dry-run, and transport failure.
Update docs for any new routing configuration knobs.
